task_name: Sandbox target and GitHub write readiness probe
goal: Prepare Phase B safely by adding a dedicated bluepilot-sandbox target profile and a guarded POST /probe/sandbox-write-check endpoint that proves the deployed Builder's GitHub token can write to G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox only. Do not open the Maya write kill-switch and do not perform a real Builder push.
scope_out: Do not set or document a secret value.
Do not change Render environment variables from code.
Do not modify SmartPush, orchestrator, providers, Maya gate, patch mode, or executor logic.
Do not point any default target away from soulmatch.
Do not allow the new probe to write to any repo except G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox.
Do not perform a real Builder task write; this task only proves sandbox write readiness.
claims: Builder target profiles include a new bluepilot-sandbox profile with repo G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox, branch main, explicit_scope_only, and dry_run_only/write-disabled behavior for now.
A new POST /probe/sandbox-write-check endpoint requires an explicit confirmation phrase before it attempts any write.
The endpoint reads a GitHub token from GITHUB_PAT, GITHUB_TOKEN, or GH_TOKEN, creates a small temporary file under .bluepilot/ in G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox, then deletes that same file.
The endpoint never returns or logs the token and never accepts a repository name from the request body.
The endpoint returns not_configured when no token exists, writable when create+delete succeeds, and not_writable when GitHub rejects the operation.
Unit tests mock fetch and prove success, missing-token, missing-confirmation, hard-coded repo, and token non-disclosure behavior.
assumptions: G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox exists with default branch main and was manually created by the operator.
Codex GitHub connector write access was proven separately by creating and deleting a temporary file; the deployed Render token still needs its own proof, which this endpoint provides after deploy.
Current SmartPush is still hard-coded to G-Dislioglu/soulmatch, so Phase B cannot safely use it until a later contract makes the write target explicit and sandbox-only.
The Maya kill-switch remains closed until the sandbox target and deployed token have been proven.
dod: builderTargetProfiles includes bluepilot-sandbox without changing the default soulmatch profile.
POST /probe/sandbox-write-check refuses requests without confirmation and never supports GET writes.
The write check is hard-coded to G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox and does not read repo/owner/path from user input except for generating a safe temporary filename internally.
Tests prove token values are not serialized in any response.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: A code change would be needed in opusSmartPush, orchestrator, providers, or Maya gate.
The endpoint would need to accept an arbitrary repository or path.
A secret value would need to be logged, returned, or committed.
A package or dependency change becomes necessary.
Opening the Maya write kill-switch becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Phase A is green: live dry-run reached target-profile, hardening, architect-assembly, scope, fetch, swarm, validate, claim-gate, judge, and workflow-simulation with status dry_run. Before Phase B, a safe sandbox target and deployed GitHub-token write proof are required because the current default target is soulmatch and SmartPush is still hard-coded to G-Dislioglu/soulmatch.
